steps.title: Rewrite with positive powers
steps.content:
The expression becomes
$$
\frac{y^{3/2}}{x^{13/4}}
$$
Convert the exponents to radicals:
$$
y^{3/2}=y\sqrt{y}
$$
$$
x^{13/4}=x^3\sqrt[4]{x}
$$

steps.title: Match the radical form
steps.content: The equivalent expression is $$ \frac{y\sqrt{y}}{x^3\sqrt[4]{x}} $$ The answer is D.
